content.question: To what extent does the documented socio-economic achievement gap (170-point reading gap) derive causally from the 6-/8-year selection structure itself versus from prior family/economic stratification, versus from broader school-type differentiation (grammar/vocational/technical) unrelated to selection age?
content.reduction_path: Quasi-experimental analysis isolating students at selection boundary (ages 10-12 entry versus 9th-grade entry) while controlling for family socio-economic status and prior achievement; requires longitudinal Hungarian administrative data with transparent coding of track assignment rules and mechanism.
